channel.title: Weekly Summary on TzJ's Net
channel.description: Recent content in Weekly Summary on TzJ's Net

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W14
item.description: 本周有三条任务同时推进：MIHD多模态空间转录组论文准备工作（5天，DCC负责）已经完成，包括TEDDY全量嵌入基准测试、Leiden对比实验、HD融合评估以及论文双语框架撰写，从而确定了“零样本FM嵌入融合”差异化研究的方向；TokenMonitor Linux FloatBall系统的修复工作也已完成，经过多轮迭代，从症状修复发展到固定窗口+GDK Input Shape架构重构，同时解决了PR #4中全部21个代码审查问题；Life-Copilot Discord Bot的重构工作也完成了，删除了2823行代码，引入了多CLI Agent架构，并实现了屏幕时间追踪、飞书输入渠道、MCP工具集成等多项新功能。此外，还完成了NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track中63篇论文的调研工作，以及新的Linux工作站环境的搭建。

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W13
item.description: This week’s core work focused on multi-model embedding expansion and issue triage for the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. On March 23, environment initialization was completed on the DCC HPC cluster, and historical challenges in the MIHD project were systematically documented — for the first time structuring the root cause of cross-section retrieval failure (incomparable embedding spaces) and the single-section ARI gap as formal documents. On March 29, four critical bugs in the TEDDY foundation model extraction script were successfully fixed, the first DLPFC section embedding extraction was completed, and batch runs were launched, establishing the foundation for a multi-model comparison benchmark.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W12
item.description: Achieved transformative breakthroughs across research, infrastructure, and production systems: proved Foundation Models (scGPT) essential for spatial transcriptomics zero-shot retrieval (100% vs 14% hit rate) while discovering Harmony’s fundamental batch correction limitations; designed complete error recovery pipeline reducing human demo requirements by 81% (1,740→329 samples) and fixed critical robosuite coordinate bug enabling large-scale scene generation (14,500+ scenes); replaced CalendarPro’s single-intent bottleneck with multi-intent classifier architecture eliminating 7-layer keyword hacks; implemented QCVLA improvement proposals via FLARE cross-project analysis; stabilized VLA inference system and separated training loss monitoring; built comprehensive academic profiling capability through Yuke Zhu 100-paper deep-wide analysis with 47-chunk segmentation; executed major gadget infrastructure overhaul with 5-mode research system, git history cleanup (94MB→516KB), unified Hugo staging architecture, and 30+ days historical report consolidation; optimized MIHD codebase eliminating O(N²) bottlenecks and duplicate code.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W11
item.description: Transformative week spanning ML infrastructure, spatial transcriptomics, robotics research, and developer tooling. Achieved major breakthroughs: completed MIHD core algorithms with cross-sample evaluation (RM-IDEAL, CrossModalEnhancer), resolved critical VLA training bugs (56% gradient dilution, 33% speedup via dependency alignment), redesigned Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 architecture, built comprehensive academic researcher profiling toolkit with multi-source integration, designed 4-group manipulation progress prediction framework for pi05, executed 7-phase CalendarPro optimization with parallel agents, unified gadget tools with centralized common/ package eliminating 400+ lines of duplication, and developed production-grade GPU monitoring for Kubernetes. Established foundation for next-generation spatial biology analysis and robot learning evaluation.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W10
item.description: Intensive multi-project research and development week spanning spatial transcriptomics evaluation infrastructure, robotics training pipelines, personal automation systems, research tooling, and application development. Major breakthroughs: resolved critical BC-RNN evaluation reliability crisis (fixed 4 cascading bugs making metrics trustworthy), completed MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark revealing layer-specific performance patterns and STAIG architectural limitations, achieved Desktop Video production-grade stability (eliminated 100% bookmark restoration failures + implemented webpage wallpaper functionality), optimized Phoenix GPU utilization 5%→80%+ via HDF5 preloading, and built CalendarPro autonomous butler system with context-aware intent classification (360 tests passing). Established large-scale training infrastructure monitoring 10+ concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA jobs while launching FLARE D0×4 training. Developed research scout tool from scratch with bilingual support and two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline. Systematically addressed technical debt through comprehensive code audits, documentation synchronization, and testing.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W09
item.description: Intensive multi-device development week spanning spatial transcriptomics pipeline optimization, robot learning benchmarking, and macOS application development. Major breakthroughs included fixing a critical ML checkpoint loading bug improving clustering metrics by 44%, establishing comprehensive robot learning baselines revealing severe distribution shift in error recovery tasks (BC-RNN 98% on normal tasks vs 0.28% on error recovery), implementing production-ready macOS features with native design patterns, and completing large-scale codebase modernization (250K+ LOC refactoring). Successfully unified multi-modal annotation sources for VisiumHD analysis, achieved 100-500x speedup through systematic optimization, and delivered Desktop Video 4.1 with critical playback bugs fixed.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W08
item.description: Intensive week advancing two major research projects: Error Recovery Benchmark transitioned from infrastructure (Phase I) to experimentation (Phase II) with VLA integration, natural error capture (271 scenarios), and baseline evaluation pipeline; MIHD spatial transcriptomics achieved critical debugging breakthroughs (coordinate system fix improving ARI 86%), architectural innovations (Vision Refinement two-stage fusion, embedding cache), and comprehensive documentation. Both projects now operationally ready for large-scale experimentation despite cluster access challenges.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W07
item.description: Intensive multi-project development week spanning spatial transcriptomics benchmarking, error recovery policy framework design, and developer tool enhancements. Completed PCA+UNI+STAIG fusion benchmarks across 7 DLPFC sections with enhanced metric visualizations, designed comprehensive 7-phase MIHD enhancement plan integrating Q-Former fusion and QueST-inspired features, expanded error recovery scene library by 293% (30→118 scenes) and fixed critical visualization bugs, implemented atomic writes and multi-device sync for gadget/summarize tool, refactored CalendarPro with recurring task support, and published historical reports to Hugo website. Key technical achievements include validating checkpoint recovery mechanisms in production, solving OSC controller compensation issues in robotic simulation, and establishing config-driven hyperparameter control patterns across multiple projects.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W06
item.description: Intensive week on MIHD spatial transcriptomics: discovered and fixed critical GCN+STAIG fusion bugs (6 issues including algorithmic alignment and hyperparameter mismatches causing 9x performance degradation), implemented RM-Ideal ground-truth evaluation framework, built public benchmark submission pipeline with ethical consent practices. Major learning: implementation bugs vs tuning issues require reference code comparison first.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W05
item.description: Successfully debugged and resolved critical MIHD spatial transcriptomics model performance regression (ARI 0.4→0.13), restoring performance to 0.28-0.45 through systematic root cause analysis. Identified environment configuration and clustering method (mclust vs kmeans) as critical factors, fixed gene feature dimensionality mismatch (3000D→50D PCA), and aligned GCN implementation with original STAIG paper architecture. Established foundation for future MIHD-STAIG integration work.

item.title: Weekly summaries for the W40 edition in 2025.
item.description: This week (2025-W40) only has valid session records for the day October 2nd. This session remained at the initialization stage. The user opened the core tracking and segmentation files of the AutoSeg-SAM2 project. However, the user did not proceed to any substantial development work. Therefore, overall this week there was no substantial progress or output.
